description: Fix 5 bugs in import-learnings: no-op --reset-stats, no-op --learnings-only, missing transaction, task_id FK violation, unnecessary MD5 dedup.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash commits optional.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases. Tests verify boundaries work correctly
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS - Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Ship solid working code first, then use tests to improve it
Test the boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: test_import_does_not_carry_task_id: Import learning with task_id='NONEXISTENT-001', verify DB has task_id IS NULL
test_import_preserves_stats_when_no_reset: Import learning with times_shown=10, times_applied=5, last_shown_at/last_applied_at set; import with reset_stats=false; verify all 4 fields preserved in DB
test_import_deduplicates_within_batch: Import file with two identical title+content learnings; assert 1 imported, 1 skipped
test_import_atomicity_all_or_nothing: Import batch of 3 learnings in a transaction; verify all 3 appear in DB after success
test_format_text_stats_preserved: ImportLearningsResult with stats_reset=false, learnings_imported=3; assert output contains 'preserved from export'
test_format_text_stats_reset: ImportLearningsResult with stats_reset=true; assert output contains 'reset to zero'
Tests compile (may use #[ignore] or be expected to fail until implementation)
userStories.description: Write failing tests BEFORE implementation for all 5 bug fixes: task_id FK nullification, stats preservation, within-batch dedup, transaction atomicity, and dedup key rename. Tests should define expected behavior for the fixed code.
edgeCases: Learning with task_id referencing non-existent task: must set task_id to None
last_shown_at is None but times_shown > 0: preserve as-is (NULL datetime, non-zero count)
Duplicate learnings within same import file: detect and skip within-batch
Empty import file []: 0 imported, 0 skipped, success
DateTime format mismatch: must use %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S not RFC 3339
Import interrupted mid-batch: transaction rollback, no partial writes

failureModes.expectedBehavior: task_id set to None, import succeeds
Second duplicate skipped, count reflects 1 imported + 1 skipped

invariants: Dedup key for same title+content always produces the same result
Dedup key for different title OR content always produces different results
After successful import, DB learning count increases by exactly the number of non-duplicate learnings
After failed import, DB learning count is unchanged (transaction rollback)
userStories.notes: TDD: Write these tests first. Update existing test call sites to remove learnings_only parameter (will require implementation change first for compilation). Tests for stats preservation must use fixed datetimes, not Utc::now(). Use chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339 or NaiveDateTime to construct fixed test datetimes.

correctness: Each PRD edge case has a corresponding test
Stats preservation test uses fixed datetimes and verifies round-trip through SQLite format
style: Follow existing test patterns in tests.rs (setup_test_db helper, make_learning helper)

userStories.description: Remove the --learnings-only CLI flag, function parameter, and result struct field. Run history import was never implemented — the flag is misleading. Changes span 4 files: cli/commands.rs (arg + example), main.rs (destructure + call), import_learnings/mod.rs (param + struct + result), tests.rs (all call sites + assertions).
userStories.notes: This is a multi-file change. Update tests.rs FIRST (will fail to compile until mod.rs is updated), then mod.rs, then cli/commands.rs, then main.rs. Or do all together since they're interdependent.

style: Clean removal — no deprecation stubs, no backwards-compat shims

userStories.title: Remove --learnings-only flag

acceptanceCriteria: conn declared as mut: let mut conn = open_connection(dir)?
load_existing_keys called before conn.transaction()
Insert loop wrapped in let tx = conn.transaction()? ... tx.commit()?
record_learning called with &tx (auto-deref to &Connection)
Within-batch dedup: seen.insert(key) used to track + check in one operation
test_import_deduplicates_within_batch passes
test_import_atomicity_all_or_nothing passes
userStories.description: Make import atomic by wrapping the insert loop in a SQLite transaction. Also add within-batch dedup by converting existing_keys from a snapshot into a mutable 'seen' set that tracks keys inserted during the current batch. Pattern: load_existing_keys() before conn.transaction() (avoids mutable borrow conflict), then seen.insert(key) returns false if already present. Pass &tx to record_learning (auto-derefs to &Connection).
userStories.notes: Transaction pattern from codebase: see src/commands/skip.rs lines 59-69. load_existing_keys must be called BEFORE conn.transaction() because transaction() takes &mut conn, preventing further borrows. The LockGuard in main.rs already ensures single-writer access.

correctness: Failed import must leave DB unchanged (transaction rollback)
Within-batch duplicates must be detected and skipped
performance: Single transaction for entire batch — SQLite handles this efficiently
style: Follow conn.transaction() / tx.commit() pattern from skip.rs

userStories.title: Wrap import loop in a transaction with within-batch dedup

acceptanceCriteria: When reset_stats=false: UPDATE sets times_shown, times_applied, last_shown_at, last_applied_at from LearningExport
When reset_stats=true: no UPDATE, stats default to zero
Datetimes formatted as %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S (not RFC 3339)
format_text shows 'Bandit statistics: preserved from export' when !stats_reset && learnings_imported > 0
format_text shows 'Bandit statistics: reset to zero' when stats_reset
test_import_preserves_stats_when_no_reset passes
test_import_with_reset_stats still passes (existing test)
test_format_text_stats_preserved passes
test_format_text_stats_reset passes
userStories.description: Make --reset-stats actually control stats preservation. Default behavior (no flag): preserve stats from export via post-insert UPDATE. With --reset-stats: stats start at zero (current behavior, no UPDATE needed). After each record_learning call, if !reset_stats, execute UPDATE learnings SET times_shown, times_applied, last_shown_at, last_applied_at WHERE id = learning_id. CRITICAL: format datetimes as %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S (SQLite format), NOT to_rfc3339(). Also update format_text to show 'preserved from export' vs 'reset to zero'.
userStories.notes: The UPDATE runs inside the transaction from FIX-004. Use tx.execute() not conn.execute(). DateTime edge case: learning.last_shown_at can be None even when times_shown > 0 — preserve as-is (pass NULL to SQL).

correctness: Stats must exactly match export values after import with reset_stats=false
Datetimes must survive round-trip: export → JSON → import → DB → export
performance: One extra UPDATE per learning when preserving stats — acceptable
style: Use %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S format to match parse_datetime in models/datetime.rs

userStories.title: Implement --reset-stats properly with post-insert UPDATE
